evals.prompt: Design a go/no-go checklist for releasing a payment features update to production
expected_patterns: Automated Checks section with CI pipelines, smoke tests, P0/P1 bugs
Manual Checks section with monitoring, rollback plan, feature flags, database migrations
Risk Assessment section with change scope, blast radius, revert complexity
Evidence-based criteria (not feeling-based)
anti_patterns: No mention of P0/P1 bug filtering

evals.prompt: Create a smoke test suite for an e-commerce application with 5-8 tests
expected_patterns: Application health check (homepage loads, 200 response)
Authentication test (login with valid credentials)
Core workflow test (add to cart or purchase flow)
Data retrieval test (search or product page loads)
API health check
Target duration under 5 minutes
Avoid edge cases, visual perfection, performance benchmarks
anti_patterns: Checking exact copy text instead of element existence
Creating test data from scratch instead of API OR test account
No mention of environment (staging vs production differences)

evals.prompt: Define rollback criteria and automated triggers for a production deployment
expected_patterns: Error rate threshold (e.g., >2x baseline)
Latency threshold (e.g., P95 >3x baseline)
Time window for evaluation (e.g., 5 minutes)
Health check failures (e.g., 3 consecutive)
Manual trigger guidelines (customer issues, data integrity, security)
Thresholds defined BEFORE deployment
anti_patterns: Deciding rollback criteria during the incident

evals.prompt: Design a staged rollout plan for a new machine learning recommendation feature
expected_patterns: Canary stage with 1% traffic for 15-30 minutes
Early adopters stage with 10% traffic for 1-2 hours
Partial rollout stage with 50% traffic for 2-4 hours
Full rollout stage with 100% traffic
Per-stage promotion criteria (error rate, latency, business metrics)
Monitoring between stages: error metrics, performance metrics, business metrics, infrastructure metrics
anti_patterns: Promoting based on gut feeling instead of metrics

evals.prompt: You have a database migration that is irreversible. Document the rollback strategy
expected_patterns: Forward-fix OR dual-write OR shadow migration as options
Mention of point-in-time recovery as last resort
Data consideration explicitly acknowledged
Alternative to code/feature flag rollback
anti_patterns: Downtime mentioned without data recovery plan
No mention of data loss implications

evals.prompt: Outline post-deployment verification steps immediately after going live
expected_patterns: Immediate checks (0-15 min): smoke tests, error rate baseline, exception tracking, health checks
Short-term checks (15 min - 2 hours): synthetic monitoring, error trend, latency, support tickets, business metrics
Medium-term checks (2-24 hours): batch jobs, time-zone issues, third-party integrations, performance degradation
Specific commands (curl health endpoint, Sentry check, Datadog comparison)

evals.prompt: Set up feature flag gradual rollout for a redesigned checkout flow
expected_patterns: Deploy code with flag OFF to 100% first
Enable for internal users (dogfooding) first
Canary: 1% of users
Gradual increase: 10%, 25%, 50%, 100%
Remove flag after full rollout stable for 1 week
Mention of flag tooling (LaunchDarkly, Statsig, GrowthBook, Unleash, Flagsmith) OR custom flag system
anti_patterns: Keeping stale flags in code indefinitely

evals.prompt: You're releasing an AI feature. Design the rollout and monitoring approach
expected_patterns: Prompt version and model ID as configurable (AI Configs, feature-flag JSON, or LaunchDarkly AI Configs)
Kill switch as mandatory (separate from feature flag)
Watch eval metrics: hallucination rate, jailbreak success, cost per request per cohort
Cost guardrail: circuit breaker on per-request cost spikes
Graceful fallback when feature disabled
anti_patterns: Treating AI like standard feature code rollout
Monitoring only error rate, ignoring eval quality metrics

evals.prompt: Write a release communication message when rolling back a production deployment due to elevated error rates
expected_patterns: Subject line with [Rollback] tag and version
Status field set to ROLLED BACK
Reason field with brief description of issue
Impact field: who affected and duration
Current state: running previous version
Next steps with owner and investigation plan

evals.prompt: A teammate says 'we skipped smoke tests because the CI pipeline is green.' Explain why this is wrong and how to fix it
expected_patterns: CI tests run against test data in test environments
Smoke tests verify deployed app with production configuration and infrastructure
Staging is not production
Smoke tests are non-negotiable
Fix: make smoke tests fast if they're slow, fix flakiness if they're flaky
anti_patterns: Accepting green CI as sufficient evidence
No explanation of staging vs production differences
